mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (currently 0.11%) continues to compress the equity discount rate, supporting valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as long as the rate remains near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.11 asof t-1mo, near zero lower bound
regime_hint: any regime with FEDFUNDS near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains under 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines below 5.5% or drops more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month, markets interpret this as evidence the labor market is nearing full employment, raising near-term Fed liftoff probability and causing a negative S&P 500 reaction over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.5% or declines more than 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.5 asof t-1mo, approaching pre-crisis full-employment levels
regime_hint: labor market approaching full employment before first rate hike
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical instances where the trigger fires, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive rather than negative, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll gains (PAYEMS) occurring while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero jointly signal an approaching monetary policy inflection point; the interaction of robust labor demand with a still-accommodative Fed stance triggers rate-hike anticipation and a negative S&P 500 drift over 20 trading days, an effect not present when either signal alone is examined.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds 200k while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=141183 thousand asof t-1mo with FEDFUNDS=0.11
regime_hint: pre-liftoff zero interest rate policy with strengthening labor market
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show forward 20d SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or rank IC of the interaction term below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a mid-cycle expansion characterized by low and stable inflation, sustained growth in RSAFS retail sales alongside positive GDPC1 growth signals durable consumer demand strength, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=441363 million asof t-1mo, GDPC1=16294.7 asof t-6mo
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with low inflation (CPI growth subdued)
failure_condition: Rank IC between combined RSAFS/GDPC1 growth and forward 60d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the relationship reverses sign when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which feeds through to higher forward earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon whenever industrial production momentum is positive.
regime_hint: any regime with positive industrial production momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month INDPRO momentum and forward 60d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST housing starts of more than 10% from its trailing 12-month high signals weakening residential investment demand, an early indicator of broader consumer retrenchment, causing a negative S&P 500 reaction over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its trailing 12-month high
regime_hint: any regime, most powerful in late-cycle housing slowdown
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive median 5d forward SPX return, or rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime where UNRATE has fallen near or below the estimated natural rate of unemployment, further declines in UNRATE amplify inflation and rate-hike fears, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; the same UNRATE decline in an early-cycle recovery regime instead produces positive returns via improving demand signals.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.5% and declining for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.5 asof t-1mo near full-employment threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening near full employment ahead of first rate hike
failure_condition: Direction of the UNRATE-return relationship fails to flip sign between early-cycle and late-cycle regimes in out-of-sample testing, or rank IC < 0.03 in the late-cycle subsample over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Low nominal FEDFUNDS combined with modest CPIAUCSL inflation implies a deeply negative or near-zero real policy rate, which lowers the opportunity cost of holding equities and boosts risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon when this joint condition holds.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.11 and CPIAUCSL=235.186 asof t-2mo implying deeply negative real rate
regime_hint: sustained negative real interest rate environment
failure_condition: Rank IC between the real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY inflation) and forward 60d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.
